full_text:
April 15, 1999 

 NO. 4-98-0690 

 IN THE APPELLATE COURT 

 OF ILLINOIS 

 FOURTH DISTRICT 

 In Re: ô the Marriage of ) ô Appeal from 
 
 JUDITH E. WALKER, ) ô Circuit Court of 
 
 Petitioner-Appellant, ) ô Logan County 
 
 and ) ô No. 96D197 
 
 JAMES E. WALKER, ) 
 
 Respondent-Appellee. ) ô Honorable 
 
 ) ô Gerald G. Dehner, 
 
 ) ô Judge Presiding. 
 
 _________________________________________________________________ 

 JUSTICE McCULLOUGH delivered the opinion of the court: 

 Judith and James Walker were married on May 7, 1994. ô No children were born of the marriage. ô The marôÙriage was disôÙsolved on March 19, 1997. ô On appeal, Judith and James dispute the trial court's disposition of James' thrift incentive plan under section 503 of the Illinois Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act (Act) (750 ILCS 5/503 (West 1996)). ô The trial court awarded Judith $17,643 as her share of the marital interest in the plan. ô The trial court ordered this amount to be paid to Judith under a qualiôÙfied domesôÙtic relaôÙtions order (QDRO), effecôÙtive the date of dissoluôÙtion. ô We affirm. 
 
 On January 12, 1998, the trial court held a hearing on property distribution issues. ô On August 5, 1998, the trial court entered the suppleôÙmenôÙtal properôÙty distriôÙbuôÙtion order. ô The trial court split the nonpension marital property fairly equally. ô The trial court awarded Judith her pension. ô Judith earns $13,036 per year working full-time as a high school secreôÙtary and part-time 
 
 in a restauôÙrant. ô Judith has worked at the high school for 25ô§ years. 
 
 Finally, the trial court awarded Judith $17,643 as her share of the marital interest in James' thrift plan, to be allocated accordôÙing to a QDRO, effecôÙtive March 19, 1997. ô James had worked for Prairie Farms Dairy (PraiôÙrie Farms) for about 20ô§ years and, at the time of the January 12, 1998, hearing was a transôÙporôÙtaôÙtion coordiôÙnaôÙtor. ô EviôÙdence regardôÙing the value of the thrift plan was preôÙsentôÙed in testiôÙmony by James at the January 12, 1998, hearing and in testiôÙmoôÙny given by Kris Rosentreter, Prairie Farms' payroll coordinaôÙtor, at a separate hearing on February 9, 1998. ô AccordôÙing to James' August 4, 1997, financial affidavit, he earns approxôÙiôÙmately $48,012 per year. ô According to Rosentreter, James earned $43,200 (gross) in the fiscal year ending on SeptemôÙber 30, 1994 (FY 1994); $43,800 for FY 1995; $46,680 for FY 1996; and $48,380 for FY 1997. ô  
 
 James testified that he has contributed 2% of his salary into an \employee account\" in the pension plan for 19 years
